fields.name: ECCE
fields.description: ECC enable. This bit reset value is defined by the user option bit configuration. When set, it can be cleared by software only after writing the unlock sequence in the RAMCFG_MxECCKEYR register. Note: This bit is reserved and must be kept at reset value in SRAM1 control register.

fields.name: ALE
fields.description: Address latch enable Note: This bit is reserved and must be kept at reset value in SRAM1 control register.

fields.name: SRAMER
fields.description: SRAM erase This bit can be set by software only after writing the unlock sequence in the ERASEKEY field of the RAMCFG_MxERKEYR register. Setting this bit starts the SRAM erase. This bit is automatically cleared by hardware at the end of the erase operation.

fields.name: SEDC
fields.description: ECC single error detected and corrected Note: This bit is reserved and must be kept at reset value in SRAM1 interrupt status register.

fields.name: DED
fields.description: ECC double error detected Note: This bit is reserved and must be kept at reset value in SRAM1 interrupt status register.

fields.name: SRAMBUSY
fields.description: SRAM busy with erase operation Note: Depending on the SRAM, the erase operation can be performed due to software request, system reset if the option bit is enabled, tamper detection or readout protection regression. Refer to Table 18: Internal SRAMs features.

fields.name: ERASEKEY
fields.description: Erase write protection key The following steps are required to unlock the write protection of the SRAMER bit in the RAMCFG_MxCR register. 1) Write 0xCA into ERASEKEY[7:0]. 2) Write 0x53 into ERASEKEY[7:0]. Note: Writing a wrong key reactivates the write protection.

fields.name: ECCE
fields.description: ECC enable. This bit reset value is defined by the user option bit configuration. When set, it can be cleared by software only after writing the unlock sequence in the RAMCFG_MxECCKEYR register. Note: This bit is reserved and must be kept at reset value in SRAM1 control register.

fields.name: ALE
fields.description: Address latch enable Note: This bit is reserved and must be kept at reset value in SRAM1 control register.

fields.name: SRAMER
fields.description: SRAM erase This bit can be set by software only after writing the unlock sequence in the ERASEKEY field of the RAMCFG_MxERKEYR register. Setting this bit starts the SRAM erase. This bit is automatically cleared by hardware at the end of the erase operation.

fields.name: SEIE
fields.description: ECC single error interrupt enable

fields.name: DEIE
fields.description: ECC double error interrupt enable

fields.name: ECCNMI
fields.description: Double error NMI This bit is set by software and cleared only by a global RAMCFG reset. Note: if ECCNMI is set, the RAMCFG maskable interrupt is not generated whatever DEIE bit value.

fields.name: SEDC
fields.description: ECC single error detected and corrected Note: This bit is reserved and must be kept at reset value in SRAM1 interrupt status register.

fields.name: DED
fields.description: ECC double error detected Note: This bit is reserved and must be kept at reset value in SRAM1 interrupt status register.

fields.name: SRAMBUSY
fields.description: SRAM busy with erase operation Note: Depending on the SRAM, the erase operation can be performed due to software request, system reset if the option bit is enabled, tamper detection or readout protection regression. Refer to Table 18: Internal SRAMs features.

registers.name: M2SEAR
registers.description: RAMCFG memory 2 ECC single error address register

fields.name: ESEA
fields.description: ECC single error address When the ALE bit is set in the RAMCFG_MxCR register, this field is updated with the address corresponding to the ECC single error.

registers.name: M2DEAR
registers.description: RAMCFG memory 2 ECC double error address register

fields.name: EDEA
fields.description: ECC double error address When the ALE bit is set in the RAMCFG_MxCR register, this field is updated with the address corresponding to the ECC double error.

fields.name: CSEDC
fields.description: Clear ECC single error detected and corrected Writing 1 to this flag clears the SEDC bit in the RAMCFG_MxISR register. Reading this flag returns the SEDC value.

fields.name: CDED
fields.description: Clear ECC double error detected Writing 1 to this flag clears the DED bit in the RAMCFG_MxISR register. Reading this flag returns the DED value.

fields.name: ECCKEY
fields.description: ECC write protection key The following steps are required to unlock the write protection of the ECCE bit in the RAMCFG_MxCR register. 1) Write 0xAE into ECCKEY[7:0]. 2) Write 0x75 into ECCKEY[7:0]. Note: Writing a wrong key reactivates the write protection.

fields.name: ERASEKEY
fields.description: Erase write protection key The following steps are required to unlock the write protection of the SRAMER bit in the RAMCFG_MxCR register. 1) Write 0xCA into ERASEKEY[7:0]. 2) Write 0x53 into ERASEKEY[7:0]. Note: Writing a wrong key reactivates the write protection.

fields.name: ECCE
fields.description: ECC enable. This bit reset value is defined by the user option bit configuration. When set, it can be cleared by software only after writing the unlock sequence in the RAMCFG_MxECCKEYR register. Note: This bit is reserved and must be kept at reset value in SRAM1 control register.

fields.name: ALE
fields.description: Address latch enable Note: This bit is reserved and must be kept at reset value in SRAM1 control register.

fields.name: SRAMER
fields.description: SRAM erase This bit can be set by software only after writing the unlock sequence in the ERASEKEY field of the RAMCFG_MxERKEYR register. Setting this bit starts the SRAM erase. This bit is automatically cleared by hardware at the end of the erase operation.

fields.name: SEIE
fields.description: ECC single error interrupt enable

fields.name: DEIE
fields.description: ECC double error interrupt enable

fields.name: ECCNMI
fields.description: Double error NMI This bit is set by software and cleared only by a global RAMCFG reset. Note: if ECCNMI is set, the RAMCFG maskable interrupt is not generated whatever DEIE bit value.

fields.name: SEDC
fields.description: ECC single error detected and corrected Note: This bit is reserved and must be kept at reset value in SRAM1 interrupt status register.

fields.name: DED
fields.description: ECC double error detected Note: This bit is reserved and must be kept at reset value in SRAM1 interrupt status register.

fields.name: SRAMBUSY
fields.description: SRAM busy with erase operation Note: Depending on the SRAM, the erase operation can be performed due to software request, system reset if the option bit is enabled, tamper detection or readout protection regression. Refer to Table 18: Internal SRAMs features.

fields.name: ESEA
fields.description: ECC single error address When the ALE bit is set in the RAMCFG_MxCR register, this field is updated with the address corresponding to the ECC single error.

fields.name: EDEA
fields.description: ECC double error address When the ALE bit is set in the RAMCFG_MxCR register, this field is updated with the address corresponding to the ECC double error.

fields.name: CDED
fields.description: Clear ECC double error detected Writing 1 to this flag clears the DED bit in the RAMCFG_MxISR register. Reading this flag returns the DED value.
